skill.name: gws-slides
skill.description: Use the gws CLI to inspect Google Slides schemas and read or write presentations.
skill.summary: Manage Google Slides presentations through authenticated gws CLI commands.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, filesystem, and weak cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. Review found no prompt injection, malware, or cryptographic code; most high-severity hits are false positives from Markdown text. Residual risk remains because the skill instructs use of the authenticated gws CLI, which can read, create, and update Google Slides data.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Google Slides CLI Operations
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md documents gws slides commands and methods that can create, read, and update presentations. This is expected behavior, but it can modify Google Workspace data when valid credentials are available.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command template and API method descriptions are explicit in SKILL.md. The risk is contextual because the commands are legitimate but can affect real presentation data.

low_findings.title: Shared Skill Path Reference
low_findings.description: SKILL.md tells the agent to read ../gws-shared/SKILL.md and run gws generate-skills if it is missing. This is a benign shared-instruction dependency, but it crosses the skill directory boundary.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relative path and command are directly present on line 16. No evidence found that the path is user-controlled or used for malicious traversal.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on multiple Markdown lines. Review found metadata, headings, and CLI examples, with no cryptographic functions or weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain skill metadata, a title, and gws command text. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic usage.

low_findings.title: Network Library Finding Is API Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a Python HTTP library pattern at the batchUpdate description. The line is Google Slides API documentation, not executable Python networking code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 26 is a Markdown bullet describing a Google Slides method. No Python imports, HTTP client calls, or hardcoded external endpoint are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to use gws slides and gws schema commands. These commands are not shell-injected, but they execute external tooling with user-supplied parameters.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and intentional. The risk depends on how a calling agent validates requested resources, methods, and parameters.

content.value_statement: Teams need reliable ways to inspect and update Google Slides without manual API research. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through gws commands for presentation operations.
actual_capabilities: Shows the gws slides command pattern for Google Slides resources and methods.
Lists presentation methods for batch updates, creation, retrieval, and pages operations.
Directs users to inspect command help before calling a Google Slides method.
Uses gws schema to discover required parameters, types, and defaults.
Explains that gws schema output should shape params and JSON flags.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI and the shared Google Workspace authentication instructions.
Does not include detailed examples for every Google Slides method.
Cannot bypass Google account permissions or workspace policy controls.
Presentation updates should be reviewed before execution because they can change live files.

use_cases.title: Create presentation workflows
use_cases.description: Prepare a repeatable process for creating blank Google Slides presentations through gws.

use_cases.title: Inspect presentation data
use_cases.description: Retrieve the latest version of a presentation before planning edits or audits.

use_cases.title: Apply controlled slide updates
use_cases.description: Use schema inspection before building batch updates for approved presentation changes.

prompt_templates.title: List available slide commands
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws Slides skill to show which presentation resources and methods I should inspect before taking action.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a presentation method
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the schema for the Google Slides presentation get method and explain the required inputs in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a safe presentation read
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a read-only Google Slides request for presentation ID [presentation_id]. Do not run changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a batch update
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the batchUpdate schema and draft a cautious execution plan for these approved slide changes: [change_summary].

output_examples.input: Find how to retrieve the latest version of a presentation.
output_examples.output: The skill points to the presentations get method. Inspect its schema first, then provide the presentation ID when calling gws.

output_examples.input: Create a new blank presentation for a team meeting.
output_examples.output: The skill identifies the presentations create method. It notes that the title is used and other provided content is ignored.

output_examples.input: Prepare a batch update for approved slide edits.
output: Confirm all requests are valid before execution.
Review the planned changes because one invalid request causes the whole update to fail.

best_practices: Inspect gws help and schema output before running any presentation method.
Use read-only methods first when validating presentation IDs or permissions.
Review batch update requests carefully before applying changes to live slides.
anti_patterns: Do not run update commands without confirming the target presentation.
Do not guess required parameters when schema inspection is available.
Do not use credentials with broader Google Workspace access than the task requires.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides use of gws commands for Google Slides presentation resources and methods.

faq.question: Can it create a Google Slides presentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents the create method for creating a blank presentation with a provided title.

faq.question: Can it update existing slides?
faq.answer: Yes. The batchUpdate method can apply validated requests to a presentation.

faq.question: Does it require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill depends on the shared Google Workspace authentication and security instructions.

faq.question: Does it include every method detail?
faq.answer: No. It tells the agent to inspect gws help and schema output for exact parameters.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because authenticated commands can read or change Google Slides data.
